title: D100 Dungeon with World Builder Expansion - Session 2
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=flJTAxtk67w

Two threads in one session. First, Daniel (@dungeondive) answers viewer questions on quest generation — specifically the *Quest Reward Value* column and the *person* variable that turn the same base quest into wildly different rewards. A King (+8 to QRV) on what would otherwise be a beginner *Lost Goodies* fetch flips it into an 800-gold payout with a +10 encounter modifier; you simply *know* about that quest and choose whether to chase it. The argument: World Builder’s point is *decision points*, not railroaded escalation.

Second, partial play-through of quest two in the Tundra of Coldness, with the mapping game in front of him. The session foregrounds several quietly clever subsystems: the *strength-tested portcullis* token that blocks an exit until lifted; a *search find* that hits the treasure table and turns a junk pile into a 250-gold trinket; a pack of zombies with no loot Daniel chooses to *let escape* (and later realises he should have killed for the combat-track reward); a snake-pit trap that empties a belt slot before launching a giant snake; and the *exploding-die Mighty Blow* skill that rewards strength-50+ characters with a re-roll on natural sixes. The combat track itself sits underneath all of this — every kill ratchets towards a permanent stat or skill bump.

----

Daniel openly admits he’s probably playing combat slightly wrong on camera and treats it as *some kind of Dungeon Dive law*. How comfortable are you broadcasting a solo game while still learning its edges?
